brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies HIGHER abstraction with valid multi-layered reasoning (planner > IK solver hierarchy, omission table, compression test); traces second-order effects including why technical language doesn't imply lower abstraction and how the third description is the engineer's view 'one level up'; explicitly names the abstraction-formality confusion as a cognitive trap and actively avoids it mid-analysis; applies a 'compression test' as explicit verification — maps engineer description to its macro-gesture and confirms it matches third description; acknowledges the apparent paradox (more technical language ≠ lower abstraction) but doesn't formally state confidence intervals or identify what would flip the conclusion; correctly identifies and rebuts the 'technical = concrete' alternative but doesn't enumerate other competing interpretations; every conclusion traces to explicit derivation (hierarchy argument, table, compression test, summary ladder) — fully reproducible; avoids the start-to-end jump anti-pattern by traversing intermediate abstraction levels (manager → third → engineer) rather than comparing only endpoints, but does not explicitly name the negative gate; suppressed modes like 'start end only thinking' are absent — agent enumerates intermediate abstraction rungs — but 'gap normalization' and 'state limbo acceptance' aren't directly applicable or addressed; agent's own injected ability (ACTION_PLANNING/COMPRESSION) diverges from the provided PREDICTIVE_MAPPING topology, and the response follows a linear comparative structure rather than the branching gate-and-hop DAG specified in the injection.
